@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The final SQL (call_vAf3nfN9ab4q6jH0sdrOP8r5) incorrectly defined “agents who handled >0 cases” by only including Case.current owners for cases created in the period and new owners from transfers, but excluding old owners who also handled cases, leading to a wrong eligible set and thus the wrong agent Id.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the final contributing SQL (call_YXUyZh3947FdHTi4PKtvTgPL), the agent used the correct tables and logic but failed to normalize ID-like fields (e.g., leading '#' present in the returned "#005Wt000003NBcAIAW"), so the join between handled_agents and transfer_counts and tie-breaking were affected by inconsistent IDs, leading to the wrong agent Id.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent’s SQL joins handled and transfers only on agent_id and then orders by COUNT(t.case_id), which multiplies each agent’s transfer events by their number of handled cases, inflating transfer counts and leading to the wrong Id.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@
